curation.synopsis: Cain and the Valhallan 597th deploy to Adumbria — a tidally-locked world whose narrow terminator-zone is the Imperium's defensive line against an incoming Chaos raiding fleet — and find themselves brigaded with the Tallarn 229th, whose regimental commissar is Cain's old Schola Progenium rival Tomas Beije. The defence holds against the obvious threat from orbit; the real threat is internal, a Slaaneshi cult deep inside the planetary government working to crack a warp portal to bring something much worse than raiders through. Cain navigates Beije's petty sabotage, sweats out the narcotic-perfume of Slaanesh he last met on Slawkenberg, and ends the campaign by foiling both the cult and Beije's career in a single very uncomfortable afternoon. The novel is the third Cain memoir and one of the series' more openly comic entries.
